title: CBIC Simplifies Customs Payments: Aggregator Option Enabled for Electronic Cash Ledger
content:
# CBIC Simplifies Customs Payments: Aggregator Option Enabled for Electronic Cash Ledger

Ek important update hai! Government ne Customs Electronic Cash Ledger mein funds deposit karne ke liye Payment Aggregator ke through payment ka option announce kiya hai. This is a significant step towards enhancing ease of doing business and providing more digital payment flexibility to taxpayers.

Here are the key details:

*   **What's New:** The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has enabled payment through "payment aggregators" as an additional mode for depositing funds into the Customs Electronic Cash Ledger.
*   **Notification Details:** This change has been introduced via **Notification No. 30/2026-Customs (N.T.) dated March 24, 2026**.
*   **Amendment:** The Customs (Electronic Cash Ledger) Regulations, 2022 have been amended. Specifically, a new clause (d) has been inserted in sub-regulation (6) of Regulation 3, allowing payments "through payment aggregator". Additionally, sub-regulation (7) of Regulation 3 now explicitly includes "payment aggregator" alongside internet banking through an authorised bank.
*   **Impact:** This move aims to facilitate smoother and more flexible customs duty payments, making digital transactions easier. It also enables payment via Credit Cards, Debit Cards, and UPI for customs duty on the ICEGATE platform for the first time, along with expanded internet banking options.
*   **Effective Date:** These amendments came into force on March 24, 2026, the date of their publication in the Official Gazette.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification here](https://taxinformation.cbic.gov.in/view-pdf/1010595/ENG/Notifications)

*Published on: 26 March 2026*
